observation: Horizontal seepage-induced suffusion tests under controlled vertical stress measured seepage flux, eroded fine-particle mass and local hydraulic gradients.
claim_supported: Path-aware screening is geotechnically meaningful because suffusion initiation can be associated with non-uniform local hydraulic gradients and evolving permeability.
claim_not_supported: Calibration or quantitative validation of the proposed FEM-Dijkstra-DEM model.

observation: Permeability can alternately decrease and increase after suffusion initiation because movable fines clog and dredge soil pores.
claim_supported: A pure global critical-gradient cutoff is an insufficient standalone comparator for path/history-sensitive erosion screening.
claim_not_supported: Prediction of the Deng et al. tests or field validation.

observation: Initiation and failure hydraulic gradients increased with initial dry density and fines content.
claim_supported: Material state should bound interpretation of the activation operator and prevent universal dam-alert claims.
